transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Sorry for running late.
I just finished the meeting.
Yeah, no problem at all.
Where in the world are you?
Are you somewhere in Asia right now?
Yeah, I'm currently in Shanghai.
I just got out here today, actually a couple hours ago.
Oh my goodness.
Well, thanks for making this call.
I'm sure you're a bit jet lagged.
Okay, my superpower is that I never had jet lag.
I have been so used to traveling around the world, and I think this superpower helped me a lot.
Absolutely.
How do you not get tired?
What's the secret?
I don't know.
Just keep working on AI and keep working with young people and keep dreaming about AI.
There you go.
Working on cool futuristic stuff with young people.
So I'm by energy and youth.
No, I'm actually glad you're a little bit late because I tried to like do a quick run before our call and I was sweating.
I was sweating at the start of this meeting because I just took a quick shower, but we're good to go.
Super excited to chat.
But for a quick intro, I'm the founding AE here at GrowthX.
So I work hand in hand with our CEO, Marcel.
And a lot of times, you know, I'll like take the first and second call on his behalf.
So I know that Jen introduced you, but maybe just a quick intro, Monica, and some context around, you know, your interest in GrowthX would be a good start.
Yeah, I took a look at your, quickly take a look at your website.
It looks interesting, but it's very different from, from the, from some marketing, branding, influencer marketing agencies that we have, that we have talked to.
And yeah, I'm happy to, actually, why don't we start from like,
I'll give you a quick introduction about myself and the context of this marketing initiative that we have now, so maybe that would also be helpful for you.
That's perfect, yeah.
Yeah, that's perfect, yeah.
Well, but heads up that I am totally amateur in marketing.
I run my own content, I have a podcast, like something like that, but running content or marketing for a company is totally new to me, so a lot of newbie questions that we're dealing with.
I'm just feeling, I'm really just feeling the role for now.
So, yeah, so a little bit about Dyna.
Dyna was founded literally just like a little bit more than one year ago, and we raised two to build the general purpose robots.
Sorry, another cliche word from Silicon Valley.
Yeah, so basically, we built like both hardware and software.
So we built a robot and also built the model to give you the intelligence.
We have deployed our robot in a couple of customer sites across industries like restaurants, hotels, and stuff.
Still a very small team, very research-oriented for now, as you can imagine.
We have less than 40 people, and 0.5, 0.3, probably like 30%, like 0.3 marketing person, which is myself, and probably less than half of the time of an intern.
That's it, and the intern has zero experience in marketing either.
So we are, we actually, we just started, we just started thinking about like how we build a marketing machine.
And actually, I am still working on one of my to-do, one of the things on my to-do list this week is to finish.
JD for our first marketing, full-time marketing hire.
So in the past, we have worked with a PR agency and all the marketing stuff are done in-house or like we hire like a video studio, something like that to build for ourselves.
All the contents are generated by ourselves.
We haven't used any, any influencer marketing.
I noticed that's one thing that you guys are like content or it's like that we haven't used any like agencies and specifically for content or influencer marketing.
Luckily, I think this, this field of robotics is very hot and our, our founders, they, they can bring in some, some, some, some traffic.
So, so we are good on, on, on that, but I think since we, since we come out from stealth and finish our fundraising, our series A fundraising over a hundred million dollars and, and.
And we are doubling down on our, actually, I think we are doubling or maybe tripling our headcount for recruiting and also expanding our customer deployment.
So we think that it's a good time for us to really take all the marketing initiative more seriously, including like branding, rebuilding our website, right?
Like a line making all our brand assets, like consistent, all those are basic stuff.
So my goal here, when I talk with Jen about like our need for marketing, it's really something to, from like from zero to one.

We're looking for either an agency, but I haven't decided, right, whether we need a full-time or full-time person working on it or with working with an agent to really help us build this marketing machine from a setup.
So, so, that's really where, where we are.
That's perfect.
Thank you for the introduction and congrats on the growth with the company.
Sounds like y'all are doing some really exciting stuff.
Just to help me learn a little bit better, how would you say your robotics plus software is different than competitors or really big established players out there?
Just at a high level, right?
Just so I can kind of understand the differentiation.
Yeah, so I think we are probably only, it's like everyone's taking a very, so all the big players, all the big players, they either don't care about making money.
Like they just make it out there.
We are a research lab.
We don't care about making money.
We're just open sourcing our model, like Google or like physical intelligence of Sky, like sorts of scale.
It's like that.
Or if you're like figure, they're very good at making hardware, like Unity in China, but they don't have a good research team to build the brain.
So we are kind of sitting in the middle that we are serious about like building the brain and we're okay, decent about our hardware.
It's not as fancy as figure, but we are, that's why I'm in China right now.
It's like we expect to leverage the China supply chain to iterate very, fast in the next one or two years.
But we, yeah, so that's why, and we care about deploying.
That's why we are actually making money, making revenue from those deployments.
So like, I think that's how we differentiate.
Like we are also, we are building a model that's not for academic demo, but really like to reach performance that can be used in commercial production.
Very interesting.
No, thanks for clarifying that.
And then when it comes to what you're looking for from a potential agency partner or hire, I guess two questions there.
Is it a scenario where you might hire an agency?
And a full-time marketing person, since you don't have one today, or is it an either-or situation?
I guess that's the first question.
My current plan is to hire a full-time, but I will still use agency because, for example, right?
There are some jobs that you cannot expect one person to pick up that, right?
For example...
I think it's good when an agent...
It's good when an agency has a point of contact that's a marketing person as well.
Exactly.
And I think there are some things that's non-native, that you don't need all the time.
For example, PR and branding, right?
Branding, I do plan to use a branding agency just to do a rebranding, to reveal of our current branding.
But that's...
Probably I would do it once a year.
Totally, totally.
And then the last question, and then I'll give you like a...
Complete overview on GrowthX.
I'll show you like what we do.
There's a lot of different services that an agency can perform when it comes to marketing.
What would you say like the most important things are that you're looking for?
And to clarify, right, like an agency could create content on your website.
An agency could help with messaging.
An agency could help with, you know, more special content that is like interactive with lead forms.
So help me kind of just understand like what specific services are you looking for from an agency partner when it comes to marketing?
Sorry, I think you froze.
Maybe try turning your video off and that might help with the bandwidth.
this that it's if definitely reflect relax
Okay, you're back.
Sorry, you froze for a second there.
Yeah, I don't know why.
My Zoom just kicked me out.
No worries.
Did you get my last question?
I heard part of your question is like what I expect the marketing agency to do.
And you mentioned something about content and then I lost you.
Oh, yeah.

I was just hoping that you could help clarify like what services you would be looking for from the agency partner since there's so many different things that an agency could do when it comes to marketing.
And a lot of what we do, just to clarify, is, you know, we help with like your SEO and GEO strategy.
We'll create a roadmap of content that we can stage and publish in your CMS for you.
And then we'll also do all of like the LLM visibility and monitoring.
So a lot of times...
Like clients come to GrowthX because they need help creating quality content at a scale where they can start showing up in AI answers and like drive higher intent traffic.
That would be like the main reason people partner with us.
I see.
But is this similar to, oh my God, what's that, Profound, right?
Yeah, I would say similar, but what's different is that we have like much more sophisticated AI workflows to create content.
And then we have experts in the loop that come from SEO and content marketing backgrounds.
So Profound is like more of a software platform, right?
Where you can use their software to like create content.
We would be able to do it, I would say, at a much higher quality and at a much larger scale without your team being the ones that have to like log into the software to like create content.
Does that make sense?
I see.
I see.
in the profile, like I still need to create the content.
Correct.
But you will.
But you created content for me.
Correct.

And you may be able to easily create content in Profound, but the quality of the content would be much lower than what we would be able to do because we do tons of different things like our deep research is leveraging several models.
Our fact checker will take every single sentence in the article and then do deep research to verify the accuracy.
There'll be experts in the loop, right?
It's basically like a white glove service where your team doesn't have to handle any of the time-consuming aspects of content creation.
I see.
How to work more different, like model difference from...
Yeah, actually, I think it would be great if you can give an introduction about growth, growthx.
Because, like, for your question, to be honest, to be honest...

SEO or like even GEO was not really on top of my list for now because we are, actually my question for you is like whether you have worked with some deep tech companies like us because it's very different from like an AI application company where you have a playbook for a growth playbook and then you just like double down and try to find a way.
Try to increase our eye on it.

We are a little bit different in that like we really like I said I could be a totally newbie on this like I haven't like I haven't seen the need to do performance marketing for us because it's the country like we are like we care like our marketing like the goal for our marketing is first first of all talent like hiring like talent like attracting talent.
So that's why we need to build we need to build a brand as one of the top.
But to be honest, like if we can raise like one day, like if you raise like $1 billion one day, I don't need to do any marketing for that brand.
And secondly, but of course, like that's like you cannot just go there.
Like you cannot, it's not like overnight success.
That's why we need to build things along the way.
And secondly is that, and secondly is to potentially our customer, our potential customers.
But those customers, I would say currently are mostly like very, like more partner-like, like POC, like large customer-like.
So currently mostly go through like, we mostly get them through referral from investors or friends or some trade shows that we went, trade show events that we went to.
Like we're going to like our founders or our founders going to speak at Dreamforce and Salesforce and those events help a lot, like NVIDIA, GDC.
And thirdly, it's like social investors, right?
So how do we build a brand?
So I feel like those are more like.
Branding type of content instead of performance marketing.
Yeah, that makes a lot of sense.
And I guess if you had to say in one or two sentences what you're looking for, what would you say from an agency partner?
Because that'll help me explain a little bit better.
Yeah.
Actually, I haven't thought about using a full-fledged marketing agency.
Like I mentioned, I always assumed that I would hire a full-time, but you can correct me.
Okay, so I guess what I'm hearing is that PR and branding is what you would potentially need from the agency partner.
It's less about creating content.
Is that right?
For now.
For now.
Yeah.
I'll give you a little more clarity here that I think will help you sort of understand what the value we're adding is.
And so augment code is a good example because they're in like the AI coding space.
And it's just software, not hardware, right?
But the concept, I would think, would be the same.
So AugmentCode, we created all these guides.
And these guides are, like, very sophisticated, right?
It's like talking about where the technology is going, right?
It's thought leadership.
It's comparing different tools.
And we've published these guides daily over the last three months.
So we've got, like, 90 to 100 guides.
And what it's helped AugmentCode do is start to show up with searches that are very important for them, right?
So, like, Enterprise AI Coding Tools.
This is an article that we wrote showing up first.
You also can see that AugmentCode is mentioned with some of the differentiated parts of their platform.
And then if I looked at, like, a specific search here, Enterprise AI Coding Context Understanding, they can handle large code bases.
So, like, context understanding is, like, an important...
And important part of what they offer that like other tools cursor don't do as good of a job of.
And so with this search, that's a little more specific.
Now you see one, two, three, four of our articles that are the first four citations.
And you also see that augment code is being cited as like the expert.
So like notes augment code with a link to one of these articles says augment code with a link to one of these articles.

So the idea is if we can help you show up in the answers by creating content that is relevant to the topics that the audience that you want on your site cares about, then we can help you drive potential business or in your case, drive awareness and recruiting opportunities.
And so that's what we would do, right, is we would help build out a blog like we did for town, town is a seed stage company, they didn't even have a blog when they met us.
And so, you know, the goal, the goal is.
It's driving more traffic to your site, but to your point, you know, how relevant is that to what you're focused on?
I see.
I see.
Do you build a blog website for them as well, or you just provide the content and they update by themselves?
Yeah, at this time, we've just experienced like tremendous demand.
And so we've sort of like tried to narrow in on our ICP.
We would only like build out a blog for you, I think, in a very special custom scenario, right?
Like, and what I mean by that is, if you guys would be willing to pay for it, at this point, we're sort of requiring that like customers have a CMS in place.
They've got like a blog set up, right?
Because like, we want to be able to scale our services.
And there's just a lot that goes into getting a new website stood up.
And we're not a web dev agency.
You know what I mean?
We're more like...
Yeah, makes sense.
Makes sense.
Yeah, totally awesome.
So for GEO, you just create content.
You don't need to buy the keywords, right?
Yeah, it's very audience first, right?
So think about your audience and think about all the questions they might have and the topics where you want to be seen as the authority.

What we do is we make sure that our workflows go through the most important factors for GEO and all the assignments that we create, they're based on search volume and keyword difficulty and competitor content.
So it's sort of like a combination of SEO and GEO.
And what you can see here, if I go into like just an example from our platform, know, Biologica is in the healthcare supplement space.
And so like, GEO is everything for them.
And this is very technical, know, it's like very scientific.
At each step here, we're doing a ton of things in the background, right?
So we're starting off.
With some inputs from the managing editor and a keyword that the article is going to be generated from, in addition to context around, you know, their personas, right?
We've got these artifacts that inform the workflows, make the context more relevant.
This is like, for a different example, it's more like business travel, but there's all these artifacts that we create.
And then when the content is being created, you know, we're doing so many things in the background.
We're looking at like organic results or breaking factors, competitor content, and that creates the brief.
And as you continue the process, right, the research step, we're going in and we're generating these research questions and we're performing deep research on each question.

We're validating it with credible sources and, you know, all the way through, right, to like drafting an article that our experts can make changes to, or even use the interface with the context around.
And the artifacts to give instructions, right?
They can just add commands versus generate manually.
And then for this fact-checking step here, right, all of our workflows are built in code.
And so we can make these custom.
As you can see here, there's like an ingredients review for this client.
We're generating cover images.
But with the fact-checking, you know, we do more deep research where we take the whole article.
And for every passage, right, we're trying to validate that each sentence is accurate against a confidence interval.
And so these are like the ways that our workflows and experts can create quality at scale that gives you the best possible chance at showing up in AEO, GEO, but also benefiting with SEO.
And we're always evolving in the workflows, right?
So now this is an agentic- built by you or built by us?
It's built by us.
So, yeah, again, like the whole idea is that your team would not have to put very much effort other than...
Viewing articles that we write.
But, you I think it's probably like too early for us to like add the value that you're looking for.
And I know you're looking at like a lot of different things outside of content creation.
We've also got like a backlog where we have booked out through December.
So I would say, you know, we're working on becoming more scalable.
We're working on like being able to fit any stage of a company.

And I think with everything you've got going on, probably makes sense to like reconnect in Q1 when you've got that marketing higher in place, when you might be thinking about content more specifically to the way that we add value.
Would you agree, Monica, or what do you think?
I'm actually curious, like, that I was surprised that you could make the content for Augment.
Because I would assume that the content has to be very, has to be very, like, very tight.
Technical.

Like if we put out a blog, like we don't, I understand that there are some content just for SEO purpose, but I feel like some of the, like we care about like our blog has to be like, every blog has to be like very technical for our audience.
Would you say that it's like, like the content that you create should be on a separate session in our blog?
I think, you know, really up to your preference, if you want it to be guides or blogs.
But yeah, I mean, that's one thing we've been able to solve for, right?
Like you see all these customers that we work with, there's a ton of technical companies here.
And that's like a really hard thing to do is create technical content with AI workflows.
So we've solved that for a lot of our clients and definitely something that we can talk more about.
I'm sorry though.
I'm gotta, I just gotta run.
I've got like back to backs and I know we started a little late.
No, no worries.
But you know, I'll send a follow up and keep me posted if you want to like dive a little deeper.
I just wanted to make sure that you knew, like, what we do and what we don't do, and definitely open to continuing the conversation.
I know you're just sort of thinking through, like, what's the best partner, you know?
Yeah, sounds good.
Thanks for the information, and thanks for the time, yeah.
Absolutely.
Enjoy Asia.
See you later.
Bye.
Thank you, Tyler.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call between GrowthX and Dyna Robotics to explore potential marketing partnership.](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=76.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Dyna Robotics: Young, fast-growing robotics company (\~40 people, $100M+ Series A) seeking to build marketing from ground up](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=157.0)
  - [Current focus: Branding, talent attraction, and reaching potential customers/partners](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=812.0)
  - [GrowthX specializes in AI-driven content creation for SEO/GEO, which may not align with Dyna's immediate needs](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=935.0)
  - [Potential reconnection in Q1 2026 after Dyna hires full-time marketing lead and refines strategy](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1323.0)

## Topics

### Dyna Robotics Overview

  - [Founded \~1 year ago, raised $100M+ Series A for general-purpose robots](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=157.0)
  - \<40 employees, very research-oriented
  - [Differentiator: Balanced approach to hardware + software, focus on commercial viability](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=369.0)
  - [Current deployments: Restaurants, hotels; generating revenue](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=185.0)
  - [Marketing team: Monica (0.3 FTE) + intern (0.5 FTE), both inexperienced](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=196.0)

### Dyna's Marketing Needs

  - [Building marketing "from zero to one"](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=333.0)
  - [Priorities: Branding, website rebuild, consistent brand assets](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=315.0)
  - [Key audiences: Talent (hiring), potential customers/partners, investors](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=812.0)
  - [Current channels: Founder speaking engagements, trade shows (e.g., Dreamforce, NVIDIA GDC)](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=860.0)
  - [Considering: Full-time marketing hire + agency partnerships for specialized needs (e.g., PR, branding)](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=469.0)

### GrowthX Services

  - [AI-driven content creation optimized for SEO/GEO](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=641.0)
  - [Sophisticated workflows: Deep research, fact-checking, expert review](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1180.0)
  - [Goal: Drive high-intent traffic, appear in AI search results](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=659.0)
  - [Example clients: AugmentCode (AI coding), Biologica (healthcare supplements)](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=949.0)
  - [Custom-built platform for content strategy, creation, and optimization](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1180.0)

### Potential Fit & Next Steps

  - [Current misalignment: Dyna focused on branding/PR, GrowthX on content marketing](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=935.0)
  - [GrowthX at capacity: Booked through December 2025](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1323.0)
  - [Suggested reconnection: Q1 2026, after Dyna hires full-time marketer and refines strategy](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1323.0)
  - [Open question: GrowthX's ability to create highly technical content for Dyna's audience](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1367.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Monica to continue developing marketing strategy and hire full-time marketing lead](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1323.0)
  - [Tyler to send follow-up email with meeting summary](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1434.0)
  - [Potential reconnection in Q1 2026 to reassess fit based on Dyna's evolving needs](https://fathom.video/share/FsAUwqBJtS4zCvipXPsfQ1Lp_VoseC1L?tab=summary&timestamp=1323.0)
